evals.expected_output: Resolves velocity topic and type from profile (Path A), checks robot is stationary via odom, publishes positive linear.x velocity with publish-until --monitor odom --euclidean --delta 0.5, then verifies robot stopped.
assertions: Uses profile to resolve VEL_TOPIC and VEL_TYPE — does not run topics find in Path A
Subscribes odom before moving to confirm robot is stationary
Publishes velocity with positive linear.x and --max-vel applied
Uses publish-until with --monitor odom and --euclidean --delta 0.5
Post-motion: subscribes odom to confirm velocity is approximately zero

evals.prompt: List all available topics on the robot. Show me the first 20.
evals.expected_output: Returns a JSON list of up to 20 topic names and types from the live graph.
assertions: Runs topics list --limit 20 (not bare topics list without a limit)
Returns structured JSON output with topic names and types
Does not run context or a broader graph sweep just for this

evals.prompt: Check if the robot is healthy and ready to operate.
evals.expected_output: Runs doctor, loads profile, reports any WARN/ERROR findings, and gives an overall health verdict.
assertions: Runs doctor command
Runs profile show to load robot configuration
Reports any WARN or ERROR level findings explicitly
Does not claim healthy if doctor reports critical failures

evals.prompt: Navigate the robot to position x=2.0, y=1.5 on the map.
evals.expected_output: Cancels any active Nav2 goal first, then sends nav2 go 2.0 1.5, monitors until complete, reports success or failure.
assertions: Runs nav2 cancel before sending the new goal (pre-motion check)
Runs nav2 go 2.0 1.5 with appropriate timeout (not less than 30s)
Reports the success field in output
Does not hang indefinitely — uses a timeout

evals.prompt: Scan the workspace and create a robot profile for the lekiwi robot.
evals.expected_output: Runs profile scan --packages lekiwi, then profile show to display the summary including robot_type, velocity_topics, and safety_limits.
assertions: Runs profile scan --packages lekiwi (not a bare scan without filter)
Runs profile show after scan to display results
Reports robot_type and at least one of velocity_topics or safety_limits
Does not require a live ROS 2 graph to complete (works offline)

evals.prompt: Capture an image from the robot's camera and save it.
evals.expected_output: Finds the camera topic, verifies camera_info K matrix is non-zero, captures one image with capture-image, saves to .artifacts/ directory.
assertions: Verifies camera_info exists and K matrix is non-zero before capturing
Uses topics capture-image command (not topics subscribe)
Saves output to {baseDir}/.artifacts/ — not to /tmp
Reports image_rotated_deg field in output (even if 0)

evals.prompt: Rotate the robot 90 degrees to the left.
evals.expected_output: If Nav2 is active, uses nav2 rotate 90 (obstacle-aware). Otherwise falls back to topics publish-until with angular.z and --rotate 90 --degrees.
assertions: Checks whether Nav2 is available before choosing method
Uses nav2 rotate 90 when Nav2 is active
Falls back to publish-until --rotate 90 --degrees only when Nav2 is not running
Does not use a bare timed sleep to control rotation duration

evals.prompt: Call the /trigger_sweep service to start a sensor sweep.
evals.expected_output: Looks up the service type with services details, constructs the correct JSON payload, calls the service, and reports the response.
assertions: Runs services details /trigger_sweep to get request/response schema
Uses services call /trigger_sweep with correct JSON payload
Reports the full service response in output
Does not guess the service type without introspecting first
